up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H, 8 mM H2SO4, 215 nm UV)

yields.Yxs: experimentally determined biomass yield used to calibrate model (reported only as ratio, experimental=100% in Fig 4C); model GAM fixed at 59.81 mmol ATP/gdw h, fitted NGAM=0.45 mmol ATP/gdw h, H+/2e- =2 for both NADH dehydrogenase and cytochrome reductase

growth_records.cite: Risso, Carla et al. Genome-scale comparison and constraint-based metabolic reconstruction of the facultative anaerobic Fe(III)-reducer Rhodoferax ferrireducens. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-447. [PMC2755013]
growth_records.snippet: Three independent sets of physiological and growth data were used to determine the NGAM and the stoichiometry of the electron transport chain ... 1) acetate and fumarate, 2) acetate and Fe(III) and 3) citrate and Fe(III). Observed experimen
growth_records.notes: Experimental anaerobic batch growth condition (10 mM acetate donor / 40 mM fumarate acceptor) whose measured biomass yield was used to calibrate the R. ferrireducens genome-scale model. Absolute Yxs and qS NOT given numerically in text; Fig 4C reports experimental yield only as a normalized ratio (experimental set to 100%). Experimental growth rate stated as <0.04 h-1 for all conditions ('Observed experimental growth rates were all less than 0.04 h-1'), so mu recorded as an upper bound, not a point value. Concentrations 10 mM acetate / 40 mM fumarate from Fig 4C caption. Medium is a 'defined freshwater medium' cited from ref 51 (Lovley/Phillips-type), full composition not restated in this paper -> medium_composition_stated:false.

yields.Yxs: biomass yield from published acetate+Fe(III) data calculated using E. coli cell mass value (1e-12 g/cell); used as one of three datasets to fit NGAM/ETC stoichiometry

growth_records.cite: Risso, Carla et al. Genome-scale comparison and constraint-based metabolic reconstruction of the facultative anaerobic Fe(III)-reducer Rhodoferax ferrireducens. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-447. [PMC2755013]
growth_records.snippet: Biomass yield from published acetate and Fe(III) data [2] was calculated using the value from E. coli (10-12 g/cell). ... conditions: ... 20 mM acetate/10 mM Fe(III)NTA.
growth_records.notes: CITED DATA, not measured in this study: acetate(20 mM)+Fe(III)NTA(10 mM) biomass yield taken from Finneran et al. (reference 2, the original R. ferrireducens description) and recomputed with an assumed E. coli cell mass of 1e-12 g/cell. Used as calibration dataset 2 of 3 for the model. No absolute mu, qS or Yxs value printed in text; Fig 4C shows yield ratio only. Growth rate <0.04 h-1 (paper-wide statement).

up.method: HPLC organic-acid analysis (Aminex HPX-87H); Fe(II) by ferrozine assay

sec.units: produced (incomplete citrate oxidation under Fe(III) limitation)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: Fe(III):citrate flux ratio for complete oxidation
mfa.units: mol Fe(III) per mol citrate (in silico prediction for complete oxidation)
mfa.note: model-predicted; experimentally Fe(III) was limiting so oxidation was incomplete, yielding acetate+succinate

yields.Yxs: one of three datasets (citrate+Fe(III)) used to fit NGAM/ETC stoichiometry; absolute value not stated

growth_records.cite: Risso, Carla et al. Genome-scale comparison and constraint-based metabolic reconstruction of the facultative anaerobic Fe(III)-reducer Rhodoferax ferrireducens. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-447. [PMC2755013]
growth_records.snippet: the experimental growth of R. ferrireducens was under electron acceptor limiting conditions, which resulted in incomplete oxidation of citrate and production of acetate and succinate.
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic batch, 56 mM ferric citrate (citrate = donor/carbon, Fe(III) = acceptor). Confirmed experimentally as electron donor+carbon source (Additional file 5 growth curve on 56 mM ferric citrate). Under experimental Fe(III)-limitation citrate was incompletely oxidized, secreting acetate and succinate (measured by HPLC). In-text 'Growth conditions' methods list citrate 10 mM as donor with a separate acceptor, but the ferric-citrate sole-substrate curve used 56 mM (Additional file 5); the 56 mM ferric citrate value is captured here. The 18:1 Fe(III):citrate ratio is a model prediction, not a measurement. Growth rate <0.04 h-1 (paper-wide). No absolute mu/qS/yield printed.

up.units: mM (30 mM initial, batch; consumed over growth curve, Fig 5)
up.method: HPLC (Aminex HPX-87H, 8 mM H2SO4, 215 nm UV)

sec.units: produced (fumarate reduced to succinate; Fig 5 inset)
produced (from fumarate via oxidative branch; Fig 5 inset)

mfa.units: model constrained by measured fumarate/succinate/acetate fluxes
mfa.note: biomass yield of fumarate calculated by constraining fumarate, succinate and acetate fluxes to the experimental values; simulated yield closely matched experimental (Fig 4C patterned bars)

yields.Yxs: biomass yield on fumarate; reported only as ratio (experimental=100%) in Fig 4C validation; simulation matched experiment

product_yields.acetate: co-product of fumarate dismutation (not quantified numerically in text)
product_yields.succinate: main reduced product of fumarate dismutation (not quantified numerically in text)

growth_records.cite: Risso, Carla et al. Genome-scale comparison and constraint-based metabolic reconstruction of the facultative anaerobic Fe(III)-reducer Rhodoferax ferrireducens. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-447. [PMC2755013]
growth_records.snippet: R. ferrireducens is able to grow with fumarate as the sole electron donor, electron acceptor and carbon source ... the main products in R. ferrireducens are acetate and succinate. ... A fourth set of experimental data, obtained from a batch
growth_records.notes: Independent VALIDATION experiment: anaerobic batch on 30 mM fumarate as sole substrate (donor+acceptor+carbon) = fumarate dismutation, triplicate cultures with SD (Fig 5). Measured: fumarate consumption with concomitant succinate + acetate production (Fig 5 inset, HPLC). Model biomass yield obtained by constraining measured fumarate/succinate/acetate fluxes; simulated yield 'closely matched' experiment (Fig 4C patterned bars, ratio-normalized to 100%). Absolute mmol/gDW/h fluxes and absolute Yxs NOT printed in text. Growth rate <0.04 h-1 (paper-wide statement).

up.units: mM (15 mM initial; reduced to nitrite)

sec.units: ~80% of consumed nitrate converted to nitrite (Fig 9B/9D)

product_yields.nitrite: ca. 80% of nitrate converted to nitrite; remainder to ammonium (~60% of that predicted assimilated)

growth_records.cite: Risso, Carla et al. Genome-scale comparison and constraint-based metabolic reconstruction of the facultative anaerobic Fe(III)-reducer Rhodoferax ferrireducens. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-447. [PMC2755013]
growth_records.snippet: Growth on 0.1% glucose and 15 mM nitrate. Ammonia (0.25 g/L) was either added or omitted from the medium. ... the growth rate was higher in the ammonia-amended cultures ... It predicts a 20% faster growth rate if ammonium is added ... The m
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic batch, glucose 0.1% donor + 15 mM nitrate acceptor, paired +/-ammonia (0.25 g/L). Measured (experimental): nitrate reduced to nitrite, ~80% conversion (Fig 9B, ion chromatography). Growth rate ~20% higher with ammonia than without (experimental, Fig 9A/9C; model reproduced this). Only relative growth-rate ratios shown, no absolute mu; growth rate <0.04 h-1 (paper-wide). glucose/nitrate uptake rates not quantified numerically.

up.units: mM (20 mM initial; reduced to nitrite)

growth_records.cite: Risso, Carla et al. Genome-scale comparison and constraint-based metabolic reconstruction of the facultative anaerobic Fe(III)-reducer Rhodoferax ferrireducens. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-447. [PMC2755013]
growth_records.snippet: B) Production of nitrite. The medium contained 0.1% lactate and 20 mM nitrate, ammonia was omitted.
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic batch, lactate 0.1% donor + 20 mM nitrate acceptor, ammonia omitted (Fig 9B). Experimental nitrite production measured by ion chromatography. No absolute mu/qS/yield printed; growth rate <0.04 h-1 (paper-wide).

growth_records.cite: Risso, Carla et al. Genome-scale comparison and constraint-based metabolic reconstruction of the facultative anaerobic Fe(III)-reducer Rhodoferax ferrireducens. BMC Genomics (2009). DOI:10.1186/1471-2164-10-447. [PMC2755013]
growth_records.snippet: R. ferrireducens can use cellobiose as electron donor and carbon source. Growth curve of R. ferrireducens on 0.1% cellobiose and 0.5 mM Fe(III)NTA.
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic batch, cellobiose 0.1% donor + 0.5 mM Fe(III)NTA acceptor (Additional file 3). Model-predicted then experimentally confirmed cellobiose growth. No absolute mu/qS/yield in text; growth rate <0.04 h-1 (paper-wide). Included as a distinct experimental substrate condition. Note: two yeast papers in this batch (PMC12648720, PMC6088182) are S. cerevisiae (eukaryote) and thus out of prokaryote scope; PMC6735923 (Schneider & Klamt) is purely in silico E. coli strain-design ranking with no measured rates - none yield prokaryote experimental-rate records.
